data.title: Afarak’s Q1 2026 Output Drops 40% on Mine Sale and Maintenance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Afarak Group Plc ( (GB:AFRK) ) has shared an announcement.

Afarak Group reported a sharp 40.2% year-on-year decline in total production for the first quarter of 2026, reflecting weaker volumes across both its Speciality Alloys and FerroAlloys operations. Speciality Alloys output fell 16.4%, with processing volumes down 5.4% as the company deliberately curtailed production to manage inventories and optimise stock levels.

Mining operations were hit harder, particularly in South Africa, where output slumped 52.7% due to the prior-year comparison including a mine that was sold in mid-2025. Turkish mining volumes also dropped 20.6% because of maintenance work, indicating that both portfolio changes and operational downtime weighed on Afarak’s production profile in early 2026.

More about Afarak Group Plc

Afarak Group SE is a specialist alloy producer with a Speciality Alloys business in southern Europe and a FerroAlloys business in South Africa. The company focuses on sustainable growth in niche alloy markets and is listed on Nasdaq Helsinki and the London Stock Exchange, giving it access to both Nordic and international investors.

data.title: Lerøy Seafood Group Plans 2026 Cash Dividend of NOK 2.50 per Share
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Leroy Seafood Group ASA ( (GB:0GM2) ) has shared an announcement.

Lerøy Seafood Group ASA plans to pay a cash dividend of NOK 2.50 per share, contingent on approval at its annual general meeting scheduled for 27 May 2026. The ex-dividend date is set for 28 May 2026, the record date for 29 May 2026 and the company expects to distribute the dividend around 8 June 2026.

The proposed dividend outlines a clear timetable for shareholders and signals the company’s intent to return cash to investors, pending formal approval. The final decision on both the dividend and the exact payment timing will be confirmed after the general meeting, in line with disclosure rules on Euronext Oslo Børs.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:0GM2) stock is a Hold with a NOK48.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Leroy Seafood Group ASA stock, see the GB:0GM2 Stock Forecast page.

More about Leroy Seafood Group ASA

Lerøy Seafood Group ASA is a Norwegian seafood company operating in the aquaculture and fishing industry, with a primary focus on farming, processing and distributing salmon, trout and other seafood products to global markets. The group is listed on Euronext Oslo Børs and is a significant player in the European seafood supply chain.

data.title: EZZ Life Science Builds Strategic Partnerships as Softer Demand Weighs on Quarterly Cash Flow
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from EZZ Life Science Holdings Ltd. ( (AU:EZZ) ) is now available.

EZZ Life Science reported Q3 FY26 customer receipts of $8.85 million, a 25% decline amid softer demand on major e-commerce platforms, leading to a net operating cash outflow of $3.2 million. The company ended the quarter with $9.96 million in cash and no debt, while investing in inventory, marketing and strategic initiatives to support longer-term growth.

To strengthen distribution and market reach, EZZ signed a four-year exclusive global distribution deal with Aumake, featuring $10 million in minimum purchase commitments, and entered a strategic cooperation with JD Health to bolster omni-channel execution in China. It also upgraded key products, launched the MeTime Series and a tailored children’s product, expanded pharmacy distribution in Australia, intensified brand-building activities internationally, and reinforced its governance structure with new board appointments.

More about EZZ Life Science Holdings Ltd.

EZZ Life Science Holdings Limited is an Australia-based life science company focused on consumer health products. It develops and distributes high-quality supplements and wellness formulations through omnichannel models across Australia, New Zealand, China, the United States and other global markets, targeting premium, brand-led segments in competitive e-commerce and retail channels.

Average Trading Volume: 23,768

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Haranga Advances Lincoln and Ibel South Gold Projects Ahead of Planned Rebrand
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Haranga Resources Limited ( (AU:HAR) ) just unveiled an update.

Haranga Resources reported ongoing progress at its 100%-owned Lincoln Gold Project in California, where it has defined a first exploration target at South Spring Hill and continued dewatering works to unlock underground drilling access. The company has completed over 3,200 metres of diamond drilling aimed at converting the historical Lincoln-Comet foreign resource into a JORC-compliant estimate, leveraging approximately $90 million of existing infrastructure and permitting to accelerate the project’s advancement.

At its Ibel South Gold Project in Senegal, Haranga identified a potentially continuous 800-metre mineralised gold trend and released high-grade aircore intercepts that justify deeper reverse circulation drilling now underway. Backed by a $9.3 million cash balance and planning a rebrand to American West Gold, the company signalled a busy near-term news flow with multiple resource, exploration target and deep drilling milestones that could reshape its resource base and strengthen its position in the gold exploration sector.

More about Haranga Resources Limited

Haranga Resources Limited is an ASX- and Frankfurt-listed gold exploration company with key assets in the United States and Senegal. Its flagship Lincoln Gold Project sits in California’s historic Mother Lode Belt and benefits from substantial legacy investment, including an existing processing plant, underground decline development, surface infrastructure and key permits, positioning the asset for potential near-term development.

Average Trading Volume: 969,315

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$57.86M

An update from East Japan Railway Company ( (JP:9020) ) is now available.

East Japan Railway Company reported solid growth for the year ended March 31, 2026, with operating revenues rising 6.8% to ¥3.08 trillion and profit attributable to owners of the parent up 10.5% to ¥247.8 billion, supported by improved profitability and a higher equity ratio. Cash flow from operations increased, net assets expanded, and the annual dividend was raised from ¥60 to ¥74 per share, with a further increase to ¥84 forecast for fiscal 2027, underscoring management’s confidence in continued revenue and earnings growth as the company targets another year of record sales and stable profits.

Traders Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (JP:8704) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Traders Holdings reported a slight decline in operating revenue and profit for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, with operating revenue down 1.6% to ¥13.2 billion and profit attributable to owners of parent falling 6.7% to ¥4.2 billion. Despite the earnings dip and weaker cash flows, total assets and net assets expanded, and return on equity remained high at 22.9%, underscoring the firm’s still-solid profitability.

The company raised its annual dividend for the year to ¥40 per share and plans a further increase to ¥45 in the next fiscal year, signaling continued shareholder-return focus. Looking ahead to the year ending March 31, 2027, Traders Holdings forecasts an 18.8% rise in operating revenue and double-digit profit growth, suggesting management expects a recovery in business momentum and a stronger earnings base for stakeholders.

data.title: Enplas Delivers Strong FY2026 Earnings and Lifts Dividend, Sees Slower Profit in FY2027
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Enplas ( (JP:6961) ) has shared an update.

Enplas reported consolidated net sales of ¥42.54 billion for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, up 11.7% year on year, with operating profit rising 16.6% to ¥6.16 billion and profit attributable to owners of parent jumping 32.7% to ¥5.23 billion. The stronger earnings lifted basic earnings per share to ¥587.90 and supported a higher annual dividend of ¥90 per share, while the equity ratio remained high at 87.8% and cash flows from operating activities stayed solid despite continued investment outflows.

For the fiscal year ending March 31, 2027, Enplas forecasts net sales to grow a further 12.8% to ¥48 billion and operating profit to edge up 3.8% to ¥6.4 billion, but expects profit attributable to owners of parent to decline 4.5% to ¥5 billion, implying a modest EPS pullback to ¥553.60. The company still plans to maintain its dividend at ¥90 per share, signaling confidence in its balance sheet and cash-generating capacity even as profit growth is projected to plateau.

More about Enplas

Enplas Corporation is a Japan-based manufacturer listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange that operates under Japanese GAAP. The company focuses on precision plastics and related components for sectors such as electronics and automotive, providing high-value-added parts that support global industrial and consumer applications.

data.title: Max Co. Raises Dividend and Tightens Shareholder Return Policy
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Max Co., Ltd. ( (JP:6454) ) has shared an update.

Max Co., Ltd. has approved an increased year-end dividend of 148 yen per share for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, up from 114 yen the previous year, reflecting its focus on shareholder returns and stronger financial performance. The higher payout, to be funded from retained earnings and subject to shareholder approval in June, lifts total dividends to 6.653 billion yen and signals confidence in the company’s profitability and capital position.

The company also revised its dividend policy, raising its target ratio of dividends to net assets from 5.0% to 6.0% while maintaining a 50% dividend payout ratio based on consolidated results. Effective from the fiscal year ending March 31, 2027, this change underscores Max’s intention to enhance shareholder returns in line with improved profitability and return on equity, potentially making the stock more attractive to income-focused investors.

More about Max Co., Ltd.

Max Co., Ltd. is a Japan-based manufacturer listed on the Prime Market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange under security code 6454. The company focuses on expanding profits through sustainable growth and emphasizes stable, long-term profit distribution to shareholders as a core management priority.

An announcement from Simplex Holdings,Inc. ( (JP:4373) ) is now available.

Simplex Holdings reported strong results for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, with revenue rising 23.8% to ¥58.7 billion and profit attributable to owners of the parent climbing 35.4% to ¥10.5 billion, supported by higher operating margins and improved returns on equity and assets. The company also strengthened its balance sheet, grew cash reserves and continued active shareholder returns, including dividends adjusted for a 4-for-1 stock split, while forecasting double-digit revenue and profit growth for the year ending March 31, 2027.

Management highlighted robust cash flow from operating activities and an increase in total equity to ¥51.5 billion, even as the equity ratio eased due to balance sheet expansion and higher treasury share holdings. For the next fiscal year, Simplex expects revenue to reach ¥70.0 billion and profit attributable to owners of the parent to rise to ¥12.4 billion, signaling confidence in sustained business expansion and ongoing dividend growth in line with earnings.

More about Simplex Holdings,Inc.

Simplex Holdings, Inc. is a Tokyo Stock Exchange-listed company operating under International Financial Reporting Standards, focused on providing technology-driven financial solutions and related services to institutional clients. data.title: Kitazato Lifts Full-Year Earnings Above Forecast on Strong Fertility Product Demand
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kitazato Corporation ( (JP:368A) ) just unveiled an update.

Kitazato Corporation reported that its consolidated results for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, exceeded its earlier forecasts, with net sales and all major profit indicators coming in above expectations. Strong demand for fertility treatment-related products, especially in overseas markets like Europe and India, drove higher sales, while a favorable product mix and disciplined cost control offset increased selling and administrative investments to lift operating profit.

Non-operating results also improved versus plan, as foreign exchange gains from the depreciation of the yen more than offset one-time costs related to the company’s recent stock exchange listing. As a result, ordinary profit and profit attributable to owners of the parent not only surpassed the company’s guidance but also increased compared with the previous fiscal year, underscoring stronger-than-anticipated earnings momentum for stakeholders.

More about Kitazato Corporation

Kitazato Corporation, listed on the Prime Market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange, operates in the medical and life sciences sector, focusing on fertility treatment-related products. The company serves both domestic and overseas markets, with notable strength in regions such as Europe and India where demand for its reproductive medicine solutions has been particularly robust.
